entries.title: Fable 5 Offline Day 17: Lutnick Letter Restores Claude Mythos 5 for ~100 US Critical Infrastructure Orgs; Full Fable Restoration 'Within Days' Per Axios
entries.summary: Commerce Secretary Lutnick's June 26 letter partially lifted the Anthropic export-control ban by restoring Claude Mythos 5 for a defined set of ~100 vetted US critical-infrastructure organizations, but Fable 5 (the consumer and developer version) remains suspended worldwide as of day 17, with Pentagon/NSA sign-off still needed and Anthropic's International MD telling Seoul press that models will return 'in coming days.'

links.title: Fable 5 Still Offline as US Clears Mythos 5 for Critical Infrastructure — TechTimes
Is Fable 5 Back? Mythos Annex A Only — June 27 Update — ExplainX
Anthropic Restores Fable 5 After US Ban — Let's Data Science

entries.title: Zhipu AI's Open-Weight GLM-5.2 Matches Claude Mythos on Cybersecurity Vulnerability Detection at 1/6th the Cost, Undermining US Export-Control Rationale
entries.summary: Semgrep benchmarks (June 22) show Zhipu AI's MIT-licensed GLM-5.2 outperforms Claude Code on IDOR detection (39% vs. 32% F1) at ~$0.17 per vulnerability, while Graphistry's CyBT-CTF evaluation confirms frontier-level cybersecurity performance — and because the model is freely downloadable worldwide, the same capability class the June 12 export controls aimed to contain is now globally accessible, with Russian hacker forums already circulating jailbreak techniques.

links.title: We Have Mythos at Home: GLM 5.2 Beats Claude in Our Cyber Benchmarks — Semgrep Blog
China's Zhipu AI GLM-5.2 Matches Claude Mythos in Vulnerability Detection — Cybersecurity News
Z.ai Matches Mythos on Cybersecurity Bug-Finding — Let's Data Science

entries.title: EU AI Act Omnibus Formally Adopted by European Council on June 29, Cementing High-Risk Deadline Shift to December 2027
entries.summary: The European Council today gave final formal adoption to the AI Act omnibus amendments, officially pushing most high-risk AI compliance deadlines from August 2026 to December 2027, adding a new ban on AI nudifier apps effective December 2026, and completing the legislative process that had been pending since the Parliament's June 16 approval.

links.title: AI Act Timeline — Council of the EU (press release 29 June 2026)
EU Approves Delays and Other Amendments to Certain EU AI Act Obligations — Morgan Lewis

entries.title: arXiv 2606.28153 (ICML 2026 Oral): Attention Head Specialization Is the Mechanistic Source of Persistent Harmful Features Across Jailbreak Attacks
entries.summary: Accepted as an oral presentation at ICML 2026, this mechanistic interpretability paper provides evidence that harmful features in large language models are maintained robustly across diverse jailbreak attacks through specialized attention heads, offering a structural explanation for why safety guardrails remain bypassable even after extensive alignment training.

links.title: Robust Harmful Features Under Jailbreak Attacks: Mechanistic Evidence from Attention Head Specialization in LLMs — arXiv 2606.28153

entries.title: Colorado AI Act's Original June 30 Effective Date Passes Uneventfully; Narrower Disclosure-Only Replacement (SB 189) Becomes the Operative Law, Effective January 2027
entries.summary: Tomorrow marks the date the first comprehensive US state AI law was supposed to take effect, but it was replaced in May by SB 189 — a narrower, disclosure-focused regime that strips the original EU-style risk-management requirements including the duty of care against algorithmic discrimination — marking the strongest signal yet that the EU regulatory model is not being adopted across US states.

links.title: Colorado AI Act Amended and Effective Date Delayed — Hunton Andrews Kurth
Colorado AI Act Takes Effect — Analysis — unrot.co

entries.title: Google Restricts Meta's Use of Gemini AI Models Due to Compute Capacity Crunch; Rents Capacity from SpaceX
entries.summary: Google capped Meta's use of Gemini AI models beginning around March after Meta requested more computing capacity than Google could supply, has since restricted other clients as well, and reportedly signed a deal to rent additional compute from Elon Musk's SpaceX, highlighting acute AI infrastructure bottlenecks as hyperscaler demand surges.

links.title: Top Tech News Today June 29, 2026 — Tech Startups

entries.title: NVIDIA Launches Halos for Robotics, Billed as Industry's First Full-Stack Safety Architecture for Physical AI Systems
entries.summary: NVIDIA announced Halos for Robotics on June 22 — an open, full-stack safety system encompassing AI compute (IGX Thor), software (Halos OS), sensors, and a third-party certification inspection lab — aimed at enabling consistent safety architecture for autonomous robots operating alongside humans in factories and warehouses, with Agility Robotics as the first adopter.

links.title: NVIDIA Announces Halos for Robotics — NVIDIA Newsroom

entries.title: Anthropic v. DOD First Amendment Lawsuit: De Facto Thaw in Commercial Relations After Mythos Partial Restoration, but Litigation Remains Active on Separate Track
entries.summary: The June 26 Lutnick letter partially restoring Mythos 5 represents a commercial thaw with the Commerce Department, but Anthropic's parallel federal lawsuits (filed March 9) alleging unconstitutional First Amendment retaliation over the DOD supply-chain-risk designation remain active and on an expedited schedule, with no new rulings this week.

links.title: Anthropic-DOD Dispute Full Timeline as of June 29 — Build Fast With AI
